Committed code proof: normal-pane wheel-up entered tmux copy-mode and raised scroll_position to 15; bridge repainted older rows; wheel-down returned to bottom and exited copy-mode. No browser/xterm-native scrolling involved.

Post-regression yatsu: real tmux wheel-up enters copy-mode and repaints older rows; wheel-down returns bottom. Served SessionTerm module has scrollback:0, no scrollToBottom, and still sends wheel frames. Full browser click-through was attempted but not claimed because dev dashboard DOM readiness was flaky.

Attach-seed deadlock fixed: the layout wait now accepts whatever size the %layout-change announces (never an exact match — multi-client shared window under window-size latest makes the announced size routinely another client's); mode flips always repaint (token dedups); an unsolicited geometry change re-seeds viewers. Verified: 2514B full seed at +55ms where the broken path delivered 0 bytes in 11s; browser xterm paints real content.
Real bridge (attachViewer+forwardWheel): scrolled the OSC8 link into copy-mode; 11 proper ST-terminated closes, 0 truncated. Verified fail-on-broken by stashing the fix.

Reproduced the live-deploy incident through two real bridge instances on a scratch socket: sized viewer holds 221x63->219x63, a foreign instance with only a hidden viewer asserts its cold default on a layout event and the watched window collapses to 120x40 (exactly the deploy's stuck size); no ignore-size flag on any client.

Fixed: bridge clients start size-neutral (tmux ignore-size, flagged via in-stream command so pre-3.2 tmux degrades gracefully) and vote only while a viewer has sized them. Same script, same socket topology: watched window stays 221x63->219x63 through the foreign instance's layout-event asserts; foreign client_flags shows ignore-size.

Re-measured under the size-vote contract via the real supervisor (superviseBridges + attachViewer/resizeBridge on a scratch socket, two real session ids): watched session converges to 214x57 and never thrashes; unwatched session's warm hold is deferred at the cold size while the vote is live and converges to 214x57 within two ticks of the viewer detaching (window-truth staleness guard, so a suppressed hold retries instead of wedging).

Re-run post-commit: watched 214x57 no thrash; unwatched hold deferred under the live vote, converged within two ticks of detach.

Reproduced through the real bridge API: each tmux control client inherits every EARLIER bridge's pty master (staircase 0,1,2,3 — 6 leaked masters across 4 clients). node-pty's Linux forkpty(3) returns the master with no FD_CLOEXEC and execs without closing inherited fds. A killed bridge's pts therefore stays open inside a live sibling → no EIO → tmux never drops the dead client → its tty write blocks the whole SERVER event loop → every dashboard terminal black. This is the live outage reported in issue tmux-server-node-pty-master-fd-cc-client-client-.

note: mis-anchored: filed while the scenario declaration was uncommitted; re-filing against the committed reproduction

verdict.note: A (reproduce). Through the real bridge API on a clean tree: each tmux control client inherits every EARLIER bridge's pty master — staircase 1,2,3 across four clients, 6 leaked masters, 18 fds beyond stdio. Root cause: node-pty's Linux forkpty(3) returns the master with no FD_CLOEXEC and execs without closing inherited fds. A killed bridge's pts therefore outlives it inside a live sibling → no EIO → tmux never drops the dead client → its tty write blocks the whole tmux SERVER event loop → every dashboard terminal black. The live outage in issue tmux-server-node-pty-master-fd-cc-client-client-.

note: re-filing: the run's secondary assertion miscounted tmux's own post-exec fds as leaks; re-measuring with the corrected invariant (inherited-from-us) so A and B share identical assertions

verdict.note: A (reproduce), clean tree @ the reproduction commit. Through the real bridge API: each tmux control client inherits every EARLIER bridge's pty master — the incident's exact staircase (1,2,3 across four clients; 6 leaked masters, all 6 traced back to this process's own fd table). Root cause: node-pty's Linux forkpty(3) returns the master with no FD_CLOEXEC and execs without closing inherited fds, so a killed bridge's pts outlives it inside a live sibling → no EIO → tmux never drops the dead client → its tty write blocks the tmux SERVER's event loop → every dashboard terminal black. The live outage in issue tmux-server-node-pty-master-fd-cc-client-client-.

B (fix), measured on the committed tree. The staircase is gone: 0 inherited pty masters and 0 fds inherited from the backend, across all four clients (was 6 and 6). The control client is now transported over child_process stdio (tmux -C), which is close-on-exec, so it holds only its own stdio plus the fds tmux opens itself after exec — a killed bridge's pts can no longer outlive it inside a sibling, so the tmux server has nothing to block on. Verified alongside: all six pre-existing bridge scenarios still pass (UTF-8 flood, OSC 8, cursor restore x2, copy-mode history, size neutrality), and the backend boots and serves /api/graph with node-pty absent from the tree.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. Two real bridge instances on one socket: the watched window holds the sized viewer's 219x63, the viewer-less foreign bridge stays ignore-size neutral. The size-vote works identically over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. Wheel-up enters tmux copy-mode and reaches pre-attach history; live output frozen while scrolled; wheel-down returns to the live bottom. Copy-mode history reach is unchanged over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. OSC 8 closes survive the copy-mode repaint with their ST intact — no underline leak. With -C there is no DCS wrapper and stripDcs is gone, so the whole strip-eats-ST class is structurally absent.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. Exactly one marker row after scroll-up-then-down — the copy-mode-exit re-seed restored the real cursor. Cursor reconstruction is transport-independent.
Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. A reconnect-style full re-seed did not double the bottom UI — the cursor was restored so the relative redraw stayed coherent.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. A real control-mode flood of CJK+box+emoji broadcasts with ZERO U+FFFD and intact payload. Byte-level parsing holds over pipe stdout exactly as over the pty (raw Buffers, newline-byte split, byte-level unescape, no string round-trip).

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change, through the real API (attachViewer warm, then resizeBridge to 100x30). First frame landed 2ms after the resize (target: a few ms, << the ~320ms poll path it replaced), and tmux's window converged to exactly 100x30 when the frame landed — event-driven via refresh-client -C + %layout-change + capture-pane, no geometry poll. Resize convergence is unchanged over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change, through the real API + supervisor. A sized viewer held session A while an unwatched, hidden bridge B ran under superviseBridges for 44s (~22 ticks): B received ZERO supervisor-driven reconstructed frames (frame-header signature counted), and its window size was never re-asserted (stayed 80x24). The 154 idle bytes were B's own shell-startup %output, genuine pane output correctly forwarded — not a per-tick capture/broadcast. The no-periodic-work invariant holds identically over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change, through the real API. A hidden (no-size) connect received ZERO bytes before its first resize (no guessed prewarm frame, no timer); its first resize drew the deferred first frame and the window converged to exactly 214x57; a viewer on a session no one ever makes visible stayed blank. First-paint remains a pure, zero-timer resize event over pipe stdio.
First post-resize pane payload was the reconstructed frame; live tail followed; xterm viewport emitted zero scroll events.

Deterministic history-top probe found the capture frame strips the OSC8 open/close and retains only LINKTEXT; prior URL detection depended on wrapped shell command echo, so it was a false-positive-prone probe. This is an existing separate gap, not caused by atomic resize repaint.
